title: How to Rent a Boat in Ibiza: A Practical Guide for Visitors
topic: How to rent a boat in Ibiza
html:
Ibiza is one of the easiest places in the Mediterranean to spend a day on the water, with calm coves, clear shallows and short hops to the neighbouring island of Formentera. If you have never chartered before, the process can feel daunting, but it is more straightforward than most first-timers expect. This guide explains how to rent a boat in Ibiza, what to budget for, and how to make the most of your time at sea.

Decide what kind of day you want

Before you compare prices, picture the day itself. A couple looking for a quiet swim and a lunch at anchor has very different needs from a group of friends celebrating a birthday. The main options around Ibiza are:

  • Motorboats and RIBs: quick and nimble, ideal for reaching several coves in a single day and for the run across to Formentera.
  • Sailing yachts: slower and more relaxed, well suited to those who enjoy being under sail and do not mind a gentler pace.
  • Catamarans: stable and spacious, a popular choice for larger groups who want room to move and shaded seating.
  • Luxury motor yachts: for those wanting crew, comfort and the freedom to roam the whole coastline in style.

Matching the boat to the experience first, rather than to the lowest price, almost always leads to a better day. You can compare these categories side by side when you start your boat rental search.

Skippered or self-drive

This is the decision that shapes everything else. A skippered charter comes with a licensed captain who handles navigation, anchoring and local knowledge, leaving you free to relax. It is the right choice for larger yachts, unfamiliar waters or anyone without a boating licence.

Self-drive, also called bareboat, lets you take the helm yourself. Smaller boats can sometimes be hired without a formal licence up to a certain engine size, but rules vary, so always confirm what is permitted before you book. If you are uncertain, a skipper is well worth the modest extra cost on your first trip.

When to go and where to sail

The season runs roughly from May to October, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months. June and September often bring warm water, lighter crowds and better value. Calm mornings are usually best for crossings, as the afternoon breeze tends to pick up.

Popular destinations include Cala Bassa and Cala Comte on the west coast for sunsets, the sheltered bays near Santa Eulalia in the east, and the turquoise sandbanks of Formentera, which is only a short crossing away. Discuss your plans with the skipper or check the cruising notes before you decide to hire a boat for the day, since conditions and anchorages change with the wind.

What is usually included

Charter rates vary widely, so read each listing carefully. Many include fuel, insurance, safety equipment and sometimes snorkelling gear or a paddleboard. Others quote fuel separately or add a cleaning fee. A typical day charter also involves a refundable security deposit. When you browse boats on a marketplace, the inclusions are usually listed clearly so you can compare like with like.

A quick tip: ask what time the boat must return to port. Many full-day charters run from roughly nine in the morning until six in the evening, and late returns can incur extra charges.

Booking with confidence

Book ahead in peak season, as the best boats and skippers fill up fast. Confirm the meeting point, the number of guests allowed and the cancellation policy, especially the rules around poor weather. Bring sun protection, water, soft-soled shoes and a light layer for the breeze on the way home. A global yacht charter platform lets you read reviews from previous guests, which is one of the simplest ways to avoid disappointment.

Thinking of listing your own boat

Ibiza draws visitors all summer, and a boat that sits idle on its mooring is missed income. If you own a vessel here or anywhere else, you can list your boat and reach travellers from around the world. Setting up a listing on boathire24.com takes only a few minutes, and you stay in control of your calendar, pricing and which bookings you accept. Whether you are the traveller or the owner, the route to a great day on the water starts in the same place.
